title: Java Collections
description: Learn Lists, Sets, Maps, and collection utilities in Java

questions.question: Which interface represents an ordered collection that can contain duplicates?
questions.explanation: List preserves order and allows duplicate values.

questions.question: Which collection does not allow duplicate elements?
questions.explanation: Set collections do not allow duplicate values.

questions.question: Which class is a resizable array implementation in Java?
questions.explanation: ArrayList provides a dynamically resizing array-backed list.

questions.question: What does a Map store?
options: Only unique keys and values separately

questions.question: Which class provides a hash table-based implementation of Map?
questions.explanation: HashMap stores key-value pairs using hashing.

questions.question: Which method adds an element to the end of a List?
questions.explanation: add() is the common method used to insert into a List.

questions.question: Which method checks whether a collection contains an item?
questions.explanation: contains() returns true if the element exists.

questions.question: Which interface represents a collection of key-value mappings?
questions.explanation: Map is the interface for key-value pairs.

questions.question: Which collection maintains sorted order by natural ordering or comparator?
Which utility class provides common collection algorithms like sort?
questions.explanation: java.util.Collections provides utility methods for collection types.
